title: carya alba subsp. ovata
intro: Carya alba subsp. ovata, also known as the shagbark hickory, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The bark of the tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Carya alba subsp. ovata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and wellness. Herbal preparations of Carya alba subsp. ovata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its numerous health benefits.
